role: riido-contracts owns shared, versioned facts that more than one Riido repository must agree on at build time or black-box test time. It is a contract module, not a runtime module.
boundary_rule: Contracts may describe shared DTOs and state vocabulary. They must not import or redefine runtime adapters, stores, Terraform modules, credentials, provider CLI invocation, or deployment evidence.

owned_contexts.responsibility: task states, terminal/active classification, legal transition matrix, FSM schema version
canonical event envelope, actor/scope/event type catalog, envelope validation, pure reducer contract
provider capability model, protocol vocabulary, compatibility status, protocol-critical args, capability fingerprint
assignment state, poll action, heartbeat/event DTOs, task-event DTOs, service schema version
shared API DSL, canonical API IR, generated OpenAPI projection fixtures, drift verification
AI Agent ubiquitous language, agent/runtime/device lifecycle policy, client-facing API enum and sum-type vocabulary
distribution channel and provider routing status vocabulary shared by daemon/control-plane

non_owned_contexts.boundary: provider process execution, local API, task DB, workspace projection, store-app helper behavior
HTTP/SSE routes, authorization, assignment store actors, metrics, review seed, public AWS adapter behavior
Terraform, AWS accounts, deployment evidence, private release tooling

direction_rules: `task` may import `ir` because FSM transitions are driven by IR event types.
Runtime repositories may import tagged `riido-contracts` releases.
Contract packages must stay standard-library only.
OpenAPI artifacts are generated projections from `apicontract` IR, not SSOT.
A new shared package requires a promotion decision and `docs/migration/contracts.md` update.

loop.observation: The split-repo context map was a hand-maintained reader document even though it defines repo ownership boundaries consumed by architecture CI.
loop.hypothesis: A compact context-map manifest can generate the reader and verify ownership boundaries in public CI without introducing runtime dependencies.
loop.execute: Render context-map.md from this manifest and publish context-map evidence in the architecture-docs workflow.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on stale generated docs, missing workflow binding, malformed context ownership entries, or missing direction rules.
loop.retrospective: Repo ownership knowledge becomes executable boundary evidence instead of prose carried by memory.
